title: Re-allow the : character in container names, but warn
body:
Per discussion in #2876, our initial container name regular expression allowed the : character (more specifically, it just didn't force the match to extend to the entirety of the container name, so any name containing at least one alphanumeric character was considered valid). The Fedora Toolbox used names including the : character, so making the regex actually match the entire container name broke them.

Make : an allowed character again, but warn that it is deprecated and may be removed.

Fixes: #2876

LGTM, but rootless tests seem unhappy
@vrothberg I'm no longer certain we want to do this - I've found a few places where it breaks established parsing (for example, `--volumes-from`)

> @vrothberg I'm no longer certain we want to do this - I've found a few places where it breaks established parsing (for example, `--volumes-from`)

@mheon, I am okay with forbidding `:` :)
I agree, this breaks toolbox though, can we work with them to fix their issues.
They've already migrated away from using it, so we should be good. Closing.
